summary: The application is small and performant at current scale. Key bottlenecks for Sprint 2: (1) ingest_batting.py still uses row-by-row inserts while ingest_pitching.py uses batch, (2) no result set size limit on execute_sql() -- fetchall() could return thousands of rows, (3) page.tsx is a monolithic client component preventing SSR, (4) no connection pool tuning. The biggest near-term win is fixing batting ingestion to match pitching's batch pattern and adding a LIMIT safety net.

findings.title: ingest_batting.py still uses iterrows() -- 20-30x slower than batch
findings.detail: ingest_batting.py iterates row by row while ingest_pitching.py uses batch. For multi-season ingestion this will be significantly impacted. Same finding as TD-014.
findings.recommendation: Refactor to match ingest_pitching.py's batch pattern.

findings.title: No result set size limit on execute_sql()
findings.detail: execute_sql() calls result.fetchall() loading entire result set into memory. An LLM query without LIMIT could return thousands of rows.
findings.recommendation: Add server-side cap: rows = result.fetchmany(100) or inject LIMIT 100 if none present.

findings.title: page.tsx is a monolithic client component (189 lines)
findings.detail: Entire page is 'use client'. Header and layout could be server-rendered. Already tracked (PERF-006/TD-009) but not addressed.
findings.recommendation: Extract QueryForm and ResultsDisplay as client components. Keep page shell as server component.

findings.title: No connection pool configuration on async engine
findings.detail: Uses SQLAlchemy defaults. Should be explicitly configured for production. Same finding as TD-018.
findings.recommendation: Set explicit pool parameters and pool_pre_ping=True.

findings.title: No LIMIT injection in LLM system prompt
findings.detail: SYSTEM_PROMPT does not instruct the LLM to add LIMIT clauses. Queries without LIMIT return all matching rows, wasting bandwidth and memory.
findings.recommendation: Add instruction to SYSTEM_PROMPT: 'Always include LIMIT 50 unless the user explicitly asks for all results.'
